text: The camera view shows the right lower quadrant where the surgeon identifies adhesions from the omentum attached to the appendix. The appendix, terminal ileum, and adhesions are visible in the surgical field.

Following the transection of adhesions with the Ligasure energy device, the surgeon assesses the anatomy, revealing the ligament of Traves, the mesoappendix, and the base of the appendix located posteriorly.

The surgeon carefully protects the cecum and terminal ileum while cutting the ligament of Traves, revealing the base of the appendix. The mesoappendix, containing the blood supply to the appendix, is transected using an energy device to maintain hemostasis. The base of the appendix is exposed and prepared for transaction.

The surgeon uses a laparoscopic vascular stapler to close and transect the appendix at its base, ensuring the ileocecal valve is protected. After transection, the cavity is irrigated and aspirated, confirming hemostasis and the integrity of the staple line.
The surgeon ensures the ileocecal valve is positioned away from the surgical field. The appendix is then placed into an endocatch bag for removal.
